concerns.text: Task T016 implements 'Spearman rank correlation' (per plan.md), but FR-004 explicitly mandates 'Pearson correlation coefficients'. This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement; the task deliverable does not satisfy the spec's statistical method.

Task T017 implements 'cluster-based permutation tests' and 'Bonferroni correction', whereas FR-005 explicitly mandates the 'Benjamini-Hochberg False Discovery Rate (FDR) procedure'. The task replaces the required statistical control method with an unauthorized alternative, violating the spec.

Task T019 implements 'regressing AR-Z500 fields against PNA/NAO scalar indices' and 'spatial correlation with canonical templates'. FR-010 requires 'cross-referencing spatial patterns with established teleconnection indices'. The task changes the validation mechanism from a cross-reference check to a regression/template-matching procedure without spec authorization.

Task T005 restricts data download to '20°N-60°N, 100°E-60°W'. FR-001 and FR-009 mandate processing the 'full 1979–2023 global grid'. The task silently narrows the scope to a regional subset, violating the global scope requirement of the spec.

Task T013 adds 'linear detrending' to Z500 data. FR-003 defines Z500 anomalies strictly as 'subtracting the 1979–2023 monthly climatology'. The task introduces an unrequested transformation step that alters the statistical properties defined in the spec.
